name: Little trip to the top of the Azet pass
name_original: Petite virée en haut du col d'Azet
region_intro: Hiking around Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors offers diverse terrain within the Hautes-Pyrénées department of France. The region features mountainous landscapes, from lush valleys to higher altitudes, providing varied hiking experiences. Key natural features include pristine lakes, such as those in the Néouvielle reserve, and waterfalls like the Cascade Saint-Christau. The area's ecosystems are rich with pine trees, moss, and ferns, supporting a variety of wildlife. Best hiking trails around Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors The most popular hiking route is Mont Village – Église Saint-Calixte loop from Estarvielle, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete, offering an easy walk through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mont Village – Église Saint-Calixte loop from Mont, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This route provides similar gentle gradients and scenic views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Dill – Lion Peak loop from Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors is defined by mountainous landscapes, pristine lakes, and waterfalls, with a network of trails offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2,000 reviews. More than 23,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors?
A: The region of Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 360 routes documented on komoot. These trails cater to various preferences, from gentle strolls through picturesque villages to more demanding mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors has numerous easy trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. There are 84 easy routes available. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10532344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Village – Église Saint-Calixte loop from Estarvielle&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 4.8 km long and offers gentle slopes and scenic views.

Q: What are some challenging hiking trails in the area?
A: For experienced hikers looking for a challenge, the region offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10532610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dill – Lion Peak loop from Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors&lt;/a&gt; is a notable option, spanning 17.4 km with significant elevation gain, typically taking nearly 6 hours to complete. This trail leads through mountainous terrain, rewarding hikers with expansive views.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors?
A: Many of the trails in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6981584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Village – Église Saint-Calixte loop from Mont&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37578490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle – Loudenvielle Lake loop from Vielle-Louron&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 14.3 km trail.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore pristine lakes, including those within the Néouvielle reserve, and admire natural wonders like the Cascade Saint-Christau waterfall. The trails also pass through diverse ecosystems with pine trees, moss, and ferns. For panoramic views, consider routes that traverse mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peyresourde Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Val Louron-Azet&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical villages or churches to explore?
A: Absolutely. The commune is dotted with charming hamlets like Cazaux Dessus, Cazaux Debat, Camors, Anéran, and Mont, featuring traditional stone houses and ancient fountains. You can visit historic churches such as the 16th-century Église Saint-Calixte in Cazaux, or the Romanesque church in Anéran with its unique fresco. Mont also boasts the painted Église Saint-Barthélémy.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors?
A: The trails in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2,000 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountainous landscapes, the tranquility of the pristine lakes, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels. The rich natural features and charming historic villages are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there opportunities for winter hiking or snowshoeing?
A: Yes, during the winter months, the region transforms, offering excellent opportunities for snowshoeing. Trails leading to places like Pic du Lion and Cap de Peyrehicade become popular for winter excursions, allowing you to experience the snowy landscapes.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in this area?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10532344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Village – Église Saint-Calixte loop from Estarvielle&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28699639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Village loop from Mont&lt;/a&gt; (8.4 km), might take around 3 hours. More challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10532610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dill – Lion Peak loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take nearly 6 hours.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots recommended?
A: Many trails in Cazaux-Fréchet-Anéran-Camors offer fantastic viewpoints due to the mountainous terrain. Routes that ascend to higher elevations or cross mountain passes, such as those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peyresourde Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Val Louron-Azet&lt;/a&gt;, provide breathtaking panoramic views of the valleys and surrounding peaks.
tips: A great lake that is worth the detour for a break after descending the surrounding passes.
Loudenvielle is a charming little mountain village on the shores of Lac de Génos. Enjoying a rich cultural heritage, Loudenvielle invites you to take a pleasant break in a remarkable natural setting.

Located in the Louron valley, the Col d'Azet peaks at an altitude of 1,580 meters. This passage connects the village of Loudenvielle and that of Azet on the heights of Saint-Lary-Soulan. The ascent from Lake Génos is 7.5 kilometers for an average of 8.3% with some passages at 13%.
